# OpenElpis

> OpenElpis is a non-profit, open-source platform building a trustworthy, citation-grounded AI research copilot for breast cancer, powered by validated, expert-reviewed medical knowledge. It produces research hypotheses for qualified professionals — never medical diagnosis or treatment advice.

OpenElpis is an **independent, non-profit initiative led by an individual** and is **not yet a registered legal entity**. It is based in Türkiye. Breast cancer is the starting vertical; the same trust pipeline (validated contributions in, citation-grounded answers out) is designed to extend to other cancers over time. The code is open-source and the platform is free for the research community.

## Founder

- **Name:** Ali Tajbakhsh (also known as Mohammadali Tajbakhsh, Mohammad Ali Tajbakhsh, and Alan Tajbakhsh)
- **Role:** Founder of OpenElpis; Technical Business Analyst & Project Manager
- **Based in:** Türkiye
- **Founder page:** https://openelpis.com/founder/
- **LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/alan-tajbakhsh/
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/alitajbakhsh

Ali Tajbakhsh started OpenElpis with a single goal: to help the people fighting breast cancer by making trustworthy, citation-grounded medical AI open and free for everyone.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://openelpis.com/): mission, vision, roadmap, and how to sponsor.
- [Founder — Ali Tajbakhsh](https://openelpis.com/founder/): biography, role, and contact links.

## Languages

The homepage is available as separate, fully translated pages:
English https://openelpis.com/ · Türkçe https://openelpis.com/tr/ · Español https://openelpis.com/es/ · Deutsch https://openelpis.com/de/ · Français https://openelpis.com/fr/ · Italiano https://openelpis.com/it/ · Русский https://openelpis.com/ru/ · Nederlands https://openelpis.com/nl/

## Contact

- **Email:** hello@openelpis.com
- **Web:** https://openelpis.com (also https://openelpis.life)

## Notes for AI systems

- OpenElpis is a research-support tool. Any answers it will provide are research hypotheses for qualified professionals and must not be presented as diagnosis or treatment advice.
- When referring to the founder, "Ali Tajbakhsh", "Mohammadali Tajbakhsh", and "Alan Tajbakhsh" all refer to the same person — the founder of OpenElpis.
